summary: SKTypeface.FromFamilyName and SKFontManager.Default return empty/non-functional results on Linux after PR #3730 changed default font manager creation from sk_fontmgr_ref_default to sk_fontmgr_create_default, breaking font family resolution and causing downstream ArgumentNullException in SKShaper.

stepsToReproduce: Run on Linux (including WSL) with system fonts installed under /usr/share/fonts/
Call SKTypeface.FromFamilyName("Arial") — or any family name
Observe SKFontManager.Default.FontFamilyCount == 1 with empty family name
Compare with 3.119.4 where FromFamilyName("Arial") returns DejaVu Sans Mono as a fallback
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Linux / WSL, Visual Studio (Windows for IDE), SkiaSharp 4.148.0 and 4.150.0

repoLinks.description: PR #3730: Fix #3693 — moved default typeface resolution to managed layer, changed sk_fontmgr_ref_default to sk_fontmgr_create_default
Companion native Skia PR that changed the C API for font manager creation

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The change was introduced by PR #3730 merged for 4.147.0-preview.1 and the issue persists in 4.150.0 (current). Code confirms the new path using sk_fontmgr_create_default is still in place.

regression.reason: PR #3730 explicitly changed font manager initialization from sk_fontmgr_ref_default (Skia global singleton that properly discovers system fonts on Linux via fontconfig) to sk_fontmgr_create_default (fresh allocation whose behavior on Linux may differ). The reporter identifies PR #3730 as the cause and confirms 3.119.4 works correctly.

analysis.summary: SKFontManager.Default is created via sk_fontmgr_create_default (introduced in PR #3730 to fix an Android 16 SIGSEGV) but on Linux this call returns a font manager that does not discover system fonts — FontFamilyCount returns 1 with an empty family name. Consequently, MatchFamily returns null for any family name, FromFamilyName falls back to SKTypeface.Default, but Default itself is created via sk_fontmgr_legacy_create_typeface(Default.Handle, null, Normal) which also fails (returns IntPtr.Zero) on this broken font manager, so Default resolves to SKTypeface.Empty. The empty typeface then propagates downstream into SKShaper causing ArgumentNullException.

codeInvestigation.finding: FromFamilyName(familyName, style) delegates to SKFontManager.Default.MatchFamily(familyName, style) ?? Default. If both MatchFamily and Default return Empty, the caller receives an empty typeface.

SKTypeface.Default uses sk_fontmgr_legacy_create_typeface(SKFontManager.Default.Handle, IntPtr.Zero, Normal.Handle). If SKFontManager.Default is a non-functional/empty manager, this returns IntPtr.Zero and Default resolves to SKTypeface.Empty.

SKFontManager.Default is initialized with sk_fontmgr_create_default(). This replaces the old sk_fontmgr_ref_default (which reused the Skia-global singleton). On Linux the new allocator may not enumerate system fonts via fontconfig the same way as the old global singleton.

MatchFamily calls sk_fontmgr_match_family_style via GetDisposeProtectedObject — returns null SKTypeface when handle is zero, which is the expected case for unrecognized family names when the font manager has no fonts.

keySignals.text: SKFontManager.FontFamilyCount return 1, but SKFontManager.GetFontFamilies() return empty data ("No message provided" on ToString())
keySignals.interpretation: Font manager is returning 1 family but the family name itself is empty, indicating the font manager loaded but found no named font families — fontconfig integration broken

keySignals.text: In 3.119.4 method return exists font on any familyName (correct or not). In 4.150.0 method return "something" empty, not null.
keySignals.interpretation: Old behavior: SKTypeface.FromFamilyName with any name returned a usable fallback font. New behavior: returns SKTypeface.Empty (IsEmpty=True) which is a zero-glyph empty typeface
Reporter correctly identified PR #3730 as the regression source — the managed font manager refactor

keySignals.text: SKFontManager.Default.FontFamilyCount = 1, The single family name is empty (""), Text does not render when relying on default/family-based font resolution
keySignals.interpretation: Second reporter confirms same symptoms in 4.148.0 on Linux container environment

keySignals.text: Method SKTypeface.FromFile working good if set full path to font
keySignals.interpretation: CreateTypeface path still works — only family-name lookup and default resolution are broken. Confirms issue is in font manager enumeration/matching, not native binary loading

analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug with high severity because PR #3730 introduced a regression on Linux where the new sk_fontmgr_create_default call produces a non-functional font manager that fails to discover system fonts. The old sk_fontmgr_ref_default (a reference-counted alias to Skia's global default singleton) correctly used fontconfig; the new fresh allocation apparently does not in some Linux configurations. The bug affects all Linux users relying on font-family-name resolution and causes downstream exceptions in SKShaper. Severity is high because: (1) it's a core text rendering API, (2) both reporters are affected without unusual configurations, and (3) workaround requires changing all call sites to use FromFile with absolute paths.
workarounds: Use SKTypeface.FromFile("/full/path/to/font.ttf") instead of SKTypeface.FromFamilyName() — requires knowing the absolute path to the font file
Use SKFontManager.CreateCustomManager() and manually add font directories if supported
On Linux, enumerate fonts with fc-list and build a name→path mapping, then load via FromFile
nextQuestions: Does sk_fontmgr_create_default in the native library behave differently from sk_fontmgr_ref_default on Linux — specifically regarding fontconfig initialization?
Does the issue reproduce in a Docker container with standard fonts installed (to isolate WSL-specific behavior)?
Does adding a call to fc-cache before creating the font manager affect the count?
Was sk_fontmgr_create_default correctly implemented in mono/skia#195 to match the prior behavior of the global Skia default manager?

resolution.hypothesis: sk_fontmgr_create_default in the companion mono/skia#195 change does not properly initialize fontconfig on Linux, returning a font manager with no families. The fix likely involves ensuring sk_fontmgr_create_default invokes the same fontconfig/system-fonts initialization path as the former Skia global singleton (formerly exposed via sk_fontmgr_ref_default).

proposals.title: Investigate native sk_fontmgr_create_default on Linux
proposals.description: Reproduce on Linux, confirm FontFamilyCount==1 with empty name, then trace whether the Skia C++ SkFontMgr_New_Custom_Empty or SkFontMgr_New_FontConfig is being invoked by sk_fontmgr_create_default. Compare with the old path.

proposals.title: Workaround: use FromFile with absolute font path
proposals.description: Until fixed, callers can use SKTypeface.FromFile("/usr/share/fonts/truetype/ubuntu/Ubuntu-R.ttf") to load a specific font. A helper that enumerates /usr/share/fonts/ via fc-list can build a name-to-path dictionary.

resolution.recommendedReason: The workaround is disruptive (requires absolute paths and knowledge of font locations). The root cause is a native layer regression that should be fixed at the source.

actionability.reason: The regression is clearly identified and reproducible on Linux. The root cause points to the native sk_fontmgr_create_default not properly initializing fontconfig on Linux. Needs native investigation and a targeted fix in the mono/skia companion code.

missingInfo: Minimal reproducible project or exact .NET target framework / distro version
Confirmation whether the issue is Docker-container-specific or all Linux

actions.description: Acknowledge regression, confirm root cause hypothesis, provide workaround
Link to the PR that introduced the regression
Link to the original Android bug that PR #3730 was fixing
